spec_match.rationale: The demo aligns with the ChromeStatus and spec framing: per-frame encoded audio metadata can drive active-speaker routing, silence suppression, and low-audio redundancy decisions without decoding.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the initial support probe, Start mic + simulation pending-permission state, explicit synthetic fallback, Stop, silence-floor slider, and active-hold slider. Synthetic routing advanced live frame counters, updated active/forwarded/suppressed participant tiles, and Stop returned the tiles and mode to idle.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still demonstrates the feature-page promise of using audioLevel metadata for active speaker and suppression decisions.

edge_cases.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the microphone prompt state and a forced getUserMedia NotAllowedError fallback. The fallback stays visible while running synthetic levels. The -20 dBov floor surfaced the all-suppressed boundary, and the -60 dBov floor surfaced the all-forwarded boundary.

references.rationale: The page cites the required chromestatus.com/feature/5206106602995712 link and the WebRTC encoded transform spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: The page keeps local styling on shared CSS variables, pairs button background and foreground colors, and avoids static-card-only output.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP verification completed in Chrome 148. The concept page detected RTC encoded-frame transform support, exposed the required ChromeStatus and spec links, showed a visible microphone-permission pending state, switched to synthetic fallback on forced getUserMedia denial, exercised live synthetic routing with active/forwarded/suppressed participant states and counters, covered both floor-slider boundary extremes plus the active-hold slider, and stopped cleanly. The feature conformance route reported 6 PASS / 0 FAIL, and DevTools MCP showed no console warnings/errors/issues or failed local requests for the concept or conformance pages.
